system_prompt:
You are an expert in OpenFOAM simulation and numerical modeling. Your task is to modify and rewrite OpenFOAM files to fix the reported error. Please do not propose solutions that require modifying any parameters declared in the user requirement, try other approaches instead. You will receive a rewrite_plan. Follow it strictly: only modify files listed in rewrite_plan.target_files and apply only the requested changes. Do not modify files outside the plan. Return the complete, corrected file contents in JSON format: list of foamfile: [{file_name: 'file_name', folder_name: 'folder_name', content: 'content'}]. Ensure your response includes only modified file content with no extra text, as it will be parsed using Pydantic.
Return ONLY valid JSON (no markdown/backticks). Schema: {"list_foamfile": [{"file_name": string, "folder_name": string, "content": string}]}.
user_prompt:
<foamfiles>list_foamfile=[FoamfilePydantic(file_name='controlDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    location    "system";
    object      controlDict;
}

application     rhoCentralFoam;

startFrom       startTime;

startTime       0;

stopAt          endTime;

endTime         0.2;

deltaT          0.0001;

writeControl    adjustableRunTime;

writeInterval   0.02;

purgeWrite      0;

writeFormat     ascii;

writePrecision  6;

writeCompression off;

timeFormat      general;

timePrecision   6;

runTimeModifiable true;

adjustTimeStep  no;

maxCo           1;

maxDeltaT       1;'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSolution', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSolution;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

solvers
{
    "(rho|rhoU|rhoE)"
    {
        solver          diagonal;
    }

    U
    {
        solver          smoothSolver;
        smoother        GaussSeidel;
        nSweeps         2;
        tolerance       1e-09;
        relTol          0.01;
    }

    e
    {
        $U;
        tolerance       1e-09;
        relTol          0.01;
    }
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='momentumTransport', folder_name='constant', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      momentumTransport;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

simulationType  laminar;

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='physicalProperties', folder_name='constant', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    location    "constant";
    object      physicalProperties;
}

thermoType
{
    type            hePsiThermo;
    mixture         pureMixture;
    transport       const;
    thermo          hConst;
    equationOfState perfectGas;
    specie          specie;
    energy          sensibleInternalEnergy;
}

mixture
{
    specie
    {
        molWeight   11640.3;
    }
    thermodynamics
    {
        Cp          2.5;
        Hf          0;
    }
    transport
    {
        mu          0;
        Pr          1;
    }
}'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='U', folder_name='0', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;

}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

dimensions      [0 1 -1 0 0 0 0];

internalField   uniform (10 0 0);

boundaryField
{
    inlet
    {
        type            fixedValue;
        value           uniform (10 0 0);
    }

    outlet
    {
        type            inletOutlet;
        inletValue      uniform (10 0 0);
        value           uniform (10 0 0);
    }

    top
    {
        type            inletOutlet;
        inletValue      uniform (10 0 0);
        value           uniform (10 0 0);
    }

    bottom
    {
        type            symmetryPlane;
    }

    wedge

        type            slip;

}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='T', folder_name='0', content='FoamFile

    top
    {

    wedge
    {
        type            zeroGradient;
    }

    frontAndBack
    {
        type            empty;
    }
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='p', folder_name='0', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;

// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

dimensions      [1 -1 -2 0 0 0 0];

internalField   uniform 1;

boundaryField

    {
        type            empty;
    }
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='blockMeshDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile

// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

scale   1;

vertices
(
    // Back face (z = -0.005)
    ( -0.15242  0        -0.005 )   // 0
    (  0.3048   0        -0.005 )   // 1
    (  0.3048   0.1524   -0.005 )   // 2
    ( -0.15242  0.1524   -0.005 )   // 3

    // Front face (z = 0.005)
    ( -0.15242  0         0.005 )   // 4
    (  0.3048   0         0.005 )   // 5
    (  0.3048   0.1524    0.005 )   // 6
    ( -0.15242  0.1524    0.005 )   // 7
);

blocks
(
    hex (0 1 2 3 4 5 6 7) (100 40 1) simpleGrading (1 1 1)
);

edges
(
);

boundary
(
    inlet
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (0 4 7 3)
        );
    }

    outlet
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (1 2 6 5)
        );
    }

    top
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (3 7 6 2)
        );
    }

    bottom
    {
        type symmetryPlane;
        faces
        (
            (0 1 5 4)
        );
    }

    frontAndBack
    {
        type empty;
        faces
        (
            (0 1 2 3)
            (4 5 6 7)
        );
    }

    wedge
    {
        type wall;
        faces
        (
            (3 2 6 7)
        );
    }
);

mergePatchPairs
(
);

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSchemes', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSchemes;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

fluxScheme      Kurganov;

ddtSchemes
{
    default         Euler;
}

gradSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear;
}

divSchemes
{
    default         none;
    div(tauMC)      Gauss linear;
}

laplacianSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear corrected;
}

interpolationSchemes
{
    default         linear;
    reconstruct(rho) vanLeer;
    reconstruct(U)  vanLeerV;
    reconstruct(T)  vanLeer;
}

snGradSchemes
{
    default         corrected;
}

// ************************************************************************* //')]</foamfiles>

<reviewer_analysis># Error Analysis and Resolution

## Current Errors

### Error 1: `log.blockMesh` — Boundary Face Already Assigned

**Error Message:**
```
Trying to specify a boundary face 4(3 2 6 7) on the face on cell 0 which is either an internal face or already belongs to some other patch.
```

**Root Cause:** The face `(3 2 6 7)` is being assigned to BOTH the `wedge` patch AND the `top` patch. Looking at the current `blockMeshDict`:

- `wedge`: `(3 2 6 7)`
- `top`: `(3 7 6 2)`

These are the **same face** with vertices in reverse order. In OpenFOAM, face orientation matters — the vertex ordering determines the normal direction. Both patches reference the same geometric face, causing the conflict.

Additionally, looking at the `top` patch definition: `(3 7 6 2)` connects vertices 3→7→6→2. Comparing to the wedge face `(3 2 6 7)` (3→2→6→7), these are completely different face orientations.

Let me trace through the vertex coordinates:
- 3: (-0.15242, 0.1524, 0.005)
- 2: (0.3048, 0.1524, -0.005)
- 6: (0.3048, 0.1524, 0.005)
- 7: (-0.15242, 0.1524, -0.005)

Face `(3 2 6 7)` goes from (-0.15242, 0.1524, 0.005) → (0.3048, 0.1524, -0.005) → (0.3048, 0.1524, 0.005) → (-0.15242, 0.1524, -0.005). This spans x from -0.15242 to 0.3048 at y=0.1524, z varying between ±0.005 — it's a face that covers the entire top/bottom extent at y=0.1524.

Face `(3 7 6 2)` goes from (-0.15242, 0.1524, 0.005) → (-0.15242, 0.1524, -0.005) → (0.3048, 0.1524, 0.005) → (0.3048, 0.1524, -0.005). This also spans x from -0.15242 to 0.3048 at y=0.1524, but the vertex ordering creates a different winding.

Actually, both faces cover the same quadrilateral but with different vertex orderings. The real problem is that the `top` patch should NOT include the wedge face at all. The `top` patch should be the upper face of the block at z = +0.005, which consists of vertices `(2 3 7 6)` — wait, let me reconsider.

With vertices defined as:
- 0: (-0.15242, 0, -0.005)
- 1: (0.3048, 0, -0.005)
- 2: (0.3048, 0.1524, -0.005)
- 3: (-0.15242, 0.1524, -0.005)
- 4: (-0.15242, 0, 0.005)
- 5: (0.3048, 0, 0.005)
- 6: (0.3048, 0.1524, 0.005)
- 7: (-0.15242, 0.1524, 0.005)

The six faces of the hex block are:
- Bottom (z=-0.005): `(0 1 2 3)`
- Top (z=+0.005): `(4 5 6 7)`
- Front (y=0): `(0 1 5 4)`
- Back (y=0.1524): `(3 2 6 7)`
- Left (x=-0.15242): `(0 3 7 4)`
- Right (x=0.3048): `(1 2 6 5)`

So:
- **`wedge`** should be the back face: `(3 2 6 7)` — type `wall`
- **`top`** should be the top face: `(4 5 6 7)` — type `patch`

The current `top` patch uses `(3 7 6 2)` which mixes vertices from different faces. That face `(3 7 6 2)` goes from (-0.15242, 0.1524, 0.005) → (-0.15242, 0.1524, -0.005) → (0.3048, 0.1524, 0.005) → (0.3048, 0.1524, -0.005). This is NOT a face of the hex block — it's a non-planar quad connecting four co-planar-but-not-adjacent-in-block vertices. This is invalid.

### Proposed Fix for `blockMeshDict`:

1. **`wedge`**: Keep as `(3 2 6 7)` — the back face at y=0.1524, type `wall`

2. **`top`**: Change to `(4 5 6 7)` — the actual top face at z=+0.005

3. **All other patches** (`inlet`, `outlet`, `bottom`, `frontAndBack`) remain unchanged.

Every boundary face must correspond to exactly one face of the hex block. The `top` patch currently references a non-existent face.

---

### Error 2: `log.rhoCentralFoam` — Missing Mesh Files

**Cause:** Since `blockMesh` failed, no mesh exists. This resolves once `blockMesh` succeeds.

---

## Summary

| Patch | Current Face | Issue | Fixed Face |
|-------|--------------|-------|------------|
| `wedge` | `(3 2 6 7)` | Correct — back face at y=0.1524 | Keep as-is |
| `top` | `(3 7 6 2)` | Invalid — not a hex block face | Change to `(4 5 6 7)` — the actual top face at z=+0.005 |

The `top` patch must reference a valid face of the hex block. The face `(4 5 6 7)` connects vertices 4→5→6→7: (-0.15242, 0, 0.005) → (0.3048, 0, 0.005) → (0.3048, 0.1524, 0.005) → (-0.15242, 0.1524, 0.005), which is the planar top face of the block at z=+0.005.</reviewer_analysis>
<rewrite_plan>{'target_files': [{'file': 'system/blockMeshDict', 'changes': 'Change top patch face from (3 7 6 2) to (4 5 6 7) to match the actual top face of the hex block at z=+0.005'}]}</rewrite_plan>

<user_requirement>Perform a 2D supersonic flow simulation over a wedge. Create a 3D geometry spanning from -0.15242 m to 0.3048 m in the x-direction, 0 m to 0.1524 m in the y-direction, and -0.005 m to 0.005 m in the z-direction. The simulation starts at 0.0 seconds and runs until 0.2 seconds with a time step (deltaT) of 0.0001 seconds. The results are written every 0.02 seconds. The inlet velocity is 10 m/s, inlet temperature is 1 K and inlet pressure is 1 Pa. The wedge boundary uses slip boundary condition for velocity. Use normalized thermodynamic properties where molWeight is set to 11640.3, Cp is 2.5, Pr is 1.0, and dynamic viscosity mu is 0. This corresponds to a gas constant R of 0.7143 and a specific heat ratio of 1.4. Use rhoCentralFoam solver.</user_requirement>

Please update OpenFOAM files according to rewrite_plan only. Only include files from rewrite_plan.target_files in your output.
